questions.question: What is the primary purpose of virtual memory management in an operating system?
options: D) To provide a virtual address space
questions.explanation: Virtual memory management provides a virtual address space, allowing programs to access memory locations that may not be physically available, and managing the allocation of physical memory efficiently.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of virtual memory?
questions.explanation: Virtual memory is a dynamic system that allocates memory as needed, and it has a physical memory limitation, meaning it can only use the available physical memory.

questions.question: What is the role of the page table in virtual memory management?
options: A) To map virtual addresses to physical addresses
D) To provide a virtual address space
questions.explanation: The page table is a data structure that maps virtual addresses to physical addresses, allowing the operating system to translate virtual addresses to physical addresses.

questions.question: Which of the following is a technique used in virtual memory management to improve performance?
questions.explanation: Techniques such as the swipe file, page replacement algorithm, and disk caching are used to improve performance in virtual memory management.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the paging algorithm in virtual memory management?
options: C) To replace pages in physical memory
D) To provide a virtual address space
questions.explanation: The paging algorithm is used to replace pages in physical memory when the system runs low on physical memory, thereby managing the allocation of physical memory efficiently.
